home.heroDescNew: Upload an hour of audio and walk away with a clean, speaker-labelled transcript, a structured summary, and answers to your own questions — saved, searchable, and exportable.
home.heroDesc: Upload any audio file and get a full transcript with speaker labels, a summary, key actions, and custom AI analysis.
home.outcomeTitle: Three outputs from a single file
home.outcomeDesc: Every transcription produces a full transcript, a structured summary, and a Q&A surface you can ask anything against.
home.outTranscriptDesc: Read who said what, with timestamps you can click. Edit any line and the summary refreshes to match.
home.outTranscriptBullet1: Automatic speaker diarization with editable names
home.outTranscriptBullet2: AI suggestions identify named speakers from context
home.outSummaryDesc: Key points, decisions, and action items extracted automatically — kept in sync with your edits.
home.outSummaryBullet2: Regenerate any time with a custom angle
home.outQADesc: Pose your own questions and get cited answers — on one transcript or across several you own.
home.outQABullet1: Cited back to the source segments
home.outQABullet2: Query across multiple transcripts at once
home.beyondDesc: WhatSaid is more than a one-shot transcription tool — your results stay alive in your account.
home.beyondEditDesc: Fix mistakes, rename speakers, and the summary catches up.
home.beyondSearchDesc: Auto-titles, auto-tags, and full-text search across your history.
home.beyondShareDesc: Email a transcript — recipients can download it or claim a copy.
home.beyondExportDesc: TXT, JSON, DOCX synchronously. PDF generated in the background.
home.howItWorksDesc: Three simple steps from audio to results saved in your account.
home.stepUploadDesc: Drop an .m4a, .mp3, or .wav file. Language is auto-detected — override it if you need.
home.stepProcessDesc: Transcription, speaker labels, structured summary and Q&A — generated asynchronously.
home.stepDownloadDesc: Read, edit, ask questions, share, and export. Everything stays in your account.
home.privacyTitle: Your audio doesn't stay with us
home.privacyBody: Uploaded files are deleted immediately after processing. Only your transcripts, summaries and outputs are kept in your account — and you can delete them, or your whole account, at any time.
home.privacyBullet2: Interface available in English, Italian and French
home.privacyBullet3: Delete individual transcripts or your account at any time
home.pricingTeaserSub: Pay once, no subscription. Buy a single transcript or a credit pack — your results stay saved.
home.miniFaqDesc: Quick answers to the things that come up most. The Help centre has the full picture.
home.miniFaqA1: One credit processes one audio file and includes the full transcript, structured summary, and Q&A — all saved in your account. The number of credits depends on audio length (see the credit table on the pricing page).
home.miniFaqA2: Credits remain available in your account based on the product terms. They don't disappear after a few days.
home.miniFaqA3: Yes — TXT, JSON, and DOCX download immediately. PDF is generated in the background and saved when ready.
home.miniFaqQ4: What happens to my audio file?
home.miniFaqA4: It's deleted immediately after processing. Only your transcript, summary and outputs are kept in your account.
home.footerDesc: Premium AI transcription with speaker labels, summaries, and custom analysis. Privacy-first — audio deleted after processing.
home.heroSubline: Upload an hour of audio and walk away with a clean, speaker-labelled transcript, a structured summary, and answers to your own questions — all saved in your account.
home.outcomeSummaryCaption: Key points and actions, kept in sync.
home.outcomeQACaption: Cited answers — across one transcript or several.
home.pricingTeaserSubNew: Buy what you need. Credits stay in your account.

privacy.title: Privacy Notice
privacy.s1Title: 1. Controller and how to contact us
privacy.s1Body: WhatSaid is operated by Fabio Petito, trading as WhatSaid, the data controller for the purposes of the UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018. You can contact us at support@whatsaid.app. A postal address is available on written request to the same email. We are not required to appoint a Data Protection Officer under Article 37 UK GDPR, nor an EU/UK Article 27 representative, because WhatSaid is offered only to residents of the United Kingdom.
privacy.s2Title: 2. Where WhatSaid is available — United Kingdom only
privacy.s2Body: WhatSaid is offered exclusively to residents of the United Kingdom. Sign-up and sign-in are restricted to GB at the application level and your Paddle billing country must be set to the United Kingdom. If you travel outside the UK temporarily you may still sign in, but new accounts cannot be created from outside the UK.
privacy.s3Intro: We collect only what we need to run the service:
privacy.s3Item1: Account data — email address, display name, country (locked to GB), and authentication metadata.
privacy.s3Item2: Audio files — held temporarily in EU-region storage solely to produce a transcript, then deleted (see section 5).
privacy.s3Item3: Generated outputs — transcript, summary, key points, custom AI outputs, language metadata and tags.
privacy.s3Item4: Billing references — Paddle transaction ID, credit ledger entries and purchase history. We never see or store your card details.
privacy.s3Item5: Consent records — Reg. 37 immediate-supply consent at checkout and upload lawful-basis attestation, with timestamp and version pointer.
privacy.s3Item6: Operational data — job status, credit balance, in-app preferences, and technical data (IP address, user-agent) collected for security and abuse prevention.
privacy.s4Title: 4. How we use your data and the lawful basis
privacy.s4Intro: Each processing purpose maps to a single UK GDPR Article 6 basis:
privacy.s4Item1: Delivering transcripts, summaries and custom outputs — Article 6(1)(b) contract.
privacy.s4Item2: Managing your account, credits and purchases — Article 6(1)(b) contract.
privacy.s4Item3: Security, fraud and abuse prevention, service reliability — Article 6(1)(f) legitimate interests.
privacy.s4Item4: Keeping transaction and tax records — Article 6(1)(c) legal obligation.
privacy.s4Item5: Sending the Article 14 notice on your behalf when you email-share a transcript — Article 6(1)(f) legitimate interests.
privacy.s5Body1: Audio files are uploaded to EU-region object storage and transmitted to AssemblyAI's EU region only — the application enforces this at runtime and will refuse any non-EU endpoint. We do not retain, archive or replay your audio.
privacy.s5Body2: As soon as the transcript is produced we delete the file from our storage and instruct AssemblyAI to delete it from theirs. Only the generated text outputs and the associated job metadata are kept.
privacy.s6Intro: We retain personal data only for as long as we need it. Our automated retention job prunes data on the following horizons (configurable in the admin Retention tab):
privacy.s6Item1: Uploaded audio — deleted immediately after transcription.
privacy.s6Item2: Transcripts and generated outputs — kept while your account is active; deleted on account deletion.
privacy.s6Item3: Consent and attestation records (Reg. 37, upload lawful basis, recipient notices) — 6 years for evidentiary purposes.
privacy.s6Item4: Audit logs, edge-function logs and email delivery logs — 90 days.
privacy.s6Item5: DSR export bundles — generated on demand, removed 7 days after download link issue.
privacy.s6Item6: Billing and tax records — retained as required by HMRC (currently 6 years).
privacy.s7Intro: We rely on a small number of vetted sub-processors. Each is bound by a written contract that includes UK GDPR Article 28 obligations:
privacy.s7Item1: AssemblyAI (EU region) — speech-to-text transcription. Audio is deleted from AssemblyAI immediately after the transcript is returned.
privacy.s7Item2: Paddle.com Market Limited — merchant of record for all sales; handles checkout, billing, tax and refunds.
privacy.s7Item3: Lovable Cloud (Supabase, EU region) — managed database, authentication, file storage and edge runtime.
privacy.s8Body: Under the current configuration your personal data is processed only inside the UK or the European Economic Area. We do not knowingly transfer your data outside the UK/EEA. If that ever changes we will update this notice and rely on the appropriate Article 46 safeguards (UK International Data Transfer Agreement or Addendum).
privacy.s9Title: 9. Your rights and how to exercise them
privacy.s9Intro: Under UK GDPR you have the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict, port, and object to processing of your personal data, and not to be subject to solely automated decisions. We respond within one calendar month.
privacy.s9Item1: Access and portability — download a machine-readable export of your data from <settingsLink>Settings → Your data and rights</settingsLink>.
privacy.s9Item2: Rectification — submit a correction request from the same page.
privacy.s9Item3: Erasure — delete your account from Settings; transcripts, outputs and profile data are removed within 30 days, subject to records we are required to keep (see section 6).
privacy.s9Item4: Other rights or anything you cannot self-serve — email support@whatsaid.app.
privacy.s10Title: 10. Right to complain to the ICO
privacy.s10Body: If you are unhappy with how we have handled your personal data you can complain to the Information Commissioner's Office at <icoLink>ico.org.uk</icoLink> or on 0303 123 1113. We would appreciate the chance to fix the issue first — please write to support@whatsaid.app.
privacy.s12Body: When you upload a recording, you are the data controller of any personal data it contains, and WhatSaid Ltd is your processor for that content. Before uploading, you must have a lawful basis under UK GDPR Article 6 — your own voice only, the consent of identifiable speakers, the necessity of a contract, or another lawful ground. Where the recording contains identifiable people other than you, Article 14 requires you to inform them their voice is being transcribed, unless a 14(5) exemption applies. You accept these duties once when you accept our Terms of Service at signup, and they continue to apply to every upload. WhatSaid Ltd is data controller for service operation (account, billing, telemetry). Our sub-processors for that content are: Lovable Cloud (hosting and platform), Supabase (managed Postgres, authentication, object storage; EU region) and AssemblyAI (speech-to-text; EU region — audio deleted immediately after transcription). See the <icoLink>ICO guidance on lawful basis</icoLink> for more.
privacy.s11Body: WhatSaid uses only storage that is strictly necessary to keep you signed in, remember your language, and keep the app working. We do not use analytics, advertising or session-replay cookies. A full inventory and how to clear each item is on our <cookiesLink>cookies page</cookiesLink>. Lawful bases: PECR regulation 6 and UK GDPR Article 6(1)(f).
privacy.s12Title: 12. Your responsibilities when uploading others' voices
privacy.s12Body: When you upload a recording you are the controller of any personal data it contains. Before we process the file you must confirm a lawful basis under Article 6 — typically your own voice only, the speakers' consent, the necessity of a contract, or another lawful ground. Where the recording contains identifiable people other than you, Article 14 requires you to inform them their voice is being transcribed, unless a 14(5) exemption applies. We record this attestation per upload and store it against the job for audit. See the <icoLink>ICO guidance on lawful basis</icoLink> for more.
privacy.s13Title: 13. Notices we send on your behalf
privacy.s13Body: When you email-share a transcript with someone, we send the recipient a short Article 14 notice on your behalf so they know their personal data has been processed by WhatSaid as your processor. The recipient can contact us at support@whatsaid.app to object or request deletion. We keep a privacy-preserving record (a hashed recipient identifier) to ensure each recipient is notified only once per job.
privacy.s14Body: WhatSaid is a consumer service intended for adults. You must be at least 18 years old to create an account. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children.
privacy.s15Body: We protect your data with encrypted transport (TLS), encryption at rest, scoped database row-level security, principle-of-least-privilege access controls and routine dependency scanning. Audio is handled over encrypted channels and removed from both our storage and our transcription provider as soon as the transcript is produced.
privacy.s16Body: We may update this notice from time to time. Material changes will be notified by email to account holders and the effective date below will be updated.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.s1Body: By creating an account or using WhatSaid you agree to these Terms of Service and our <privacyLink>Privacy Notice</privacyLink>. If you do not agree, do not use the service.
terms.s2Body: WhatSaid is operated by Fabio Petito, trading as WhatSaid (a sole trader established in the United Kingdom). For any questions about these terms contact us at support@whatsaid.app. A postal address is available on written request.
terms.s3Body: WhatSaid is offered exclusively to residents of the United Kingdom. You must be at least 18 years old, have legal capacity to enter into a contract, and confirm that your billing country is the United Kingdom. Sign-up and Paddle checkout will refuse non-GB origins.
terms.s4Body: WhatSaid provides AI-powered audio transcription, summarisation and custom text analysis. Outputs are generated by artificial intelligence and may contain errors, omissions or inaccuracies. They should not be relied on as verbatim legal, medical or official records. See section 9 for the AI output disclaimer.
terms.s5Item1: Upload recordings made without the consent of recorded parties where consent is required by law.
terms.s5Item2: Use the service for surveillance, covert monitoring or any unlawful purpose.
terms.s5Item3: Upload content that infringes any applicable law or third-party rights.
terms.s5Item4: Attempt to abuse, overload, scrape, reverse-engineer or otherwise interfere with the service.
terms.s5Outro: We may suspend or terminate accounts that breach these terms.
terms.s6Title: 6. Credits, prices and Paddle as merchant of record
terms.s6Intro: All sales are processed by Paddle.com Market Limited, our merchant of record. Paddle handles checkout, billing, VAT and receipts on our behalf.
terms.s6Item1: Prices are shown in GBP and include VAT where applicable. The final amount and any tax are confirmed by Paddle at checkout.
terms.s6Item2: Credits are prepaid. Unless otherwise stated at purchase, credits remain available while your account is active and are forfeited on voluntary account deletion.
terms.s6Item3: If a job fails because of a service error, the credit is returned to your balance automatically.
terms.s6Item4: We do not see or store your payment-card details — Paddle does, under its own terms.
terms.s7Title: 7. Digital delivery and your right to cancel under the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013
terms.s7Body: WhatSaid is a digital service. Under the Consumer Contracts Regulations 2013 you would normally have 14 days to cancel a digital purchase. Regulation 37 lets you ask us to begin supply immediately and waive that right; before each purchase we present a short consent dialog, and you must tick both boxes confirming that (a) you ask us to begin supply now, and (b) you understand you will lose your right to cancel once supply begins. We store a timestamped record of that consent (version pointer and consent ID) for audit. Credits are usable as soon as your purchase is confirmed, so supply is treated as begun at that point.
terms.s8Body: Outside the Reg. 37 case above, our approach to refunds is set out in our <refundLink>Refund Policy</refundLink>. Refund handling is operated by Paddle as merchant of record.
terms.s9Body: All outputs — transcripts, summaries, speaker labels, key points and custom analyses — are generated by AI and provided "as is". WhatSaid does not guarantee accuracy, completeness or fitness for any particular purpose. Speaker identification is approximate and must not be treated as definitive attribution. You are responsible for confirming a lawful basis under UK GDPR Article 6 for each upload and, where required, informing identifiable speakers under Article 14 (see the Privacy Notice).
terms.s10Body: You retain ownership of the audio you upload and of the generated outputs. You grant us a limited, non-exclusive licence to process that content strictly for the purposes set out in the Privacy Notice (transcription, generating your outputs, providing the service to you, security and abuse prevention). WhatSaid's branding, code and technology remain owned by Fabio Petito trading as WhatSaid; you may not copy, reproduce or redistribute the service itself.
terms.s11Body: You are responsible for keeping your credentials secure. We may suspend or terminate access without notice if we reasonably believe your account is being used to breach these terms, to harm other users or systems, or in breach of applicable law.
terms.s12Body: To the maximum extent permitted by law, WhatSaid and its operator are not liable for indirect, incidental, special or consequential losses arising from your use of the service, including losses caused by AI output inaccuracies or recording-consent issues. Nothing in these terms limits or excludes our liability for death or personal injury caused by negligence, for fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation, or for any other liability that cannot be limited or excluded under English law — including your statutory rights under the Consumer Rights Act 2015 (in particular sections 31, 47, 57 and 65).
terms.s13Title: 13. Termination and what happens to your data
terms.s13Body: You may delete your account at any time from Settings. We may terminate or suspend your account for breach of these terms. On termination we delete your account data on the horizons set out in section 6 of the Privacy Notice, subject to records we are legally required to keep.
terms.s14Body: We may update these terms from time to time. Material changes affecting active accounts or existing purchases will be notified by email and the effective date below will be updated.
terms.s15Body: These terms are governed by the laws of England and Wales. Any dispute is subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.
terms.s18Body: For any question about these terms, write to support@whatsaid.app.
terms.s19Body: When you upload a recording to WhatSaid you warrant that (a) you have a lawful basis under UK GDPR Article 6 to process the audio and any personal data it contains, and (b) where the recording contains identifiable people other than yourself you will, where required, inform them that their voice is being transcribed in line with Article 14, unless an Article 14(5) exemption applies. You agree to indemnify WhatSaid against any third-party claim arising from breach of these warranties. This obligation is accepted once when you accept these Terms and continues to apply to every upload. See the Privacy Notice section on uploader duties for full detail.

s17Body: If any provision of these terms is held unenforceable, the remaining provisions remain in full force. These terms, together with the Privacy Notice and Refund Policy, form the entire agreement between you and WhatSaid in respect of the service.
s18Body: For any question about these terms, write to support@whatsaid.app.

refund.title: Refund Policy
refund.s1Body: This Refund Policy applies to purchases made from WhatSaid, operated by Fabio Petito, trading as WhatSaid. We want every purchase to deliver real value. If something goes wrong, we will work with you to make it right.
refund.s2Body: To request a refund, contact us at support@whatsaid.app with your account email and a brief description of the issue. We aim to respond within 2 business days.
refund.s3Body: Refunds may be issued if processing fails due to a service error and credits are not automatically restored, if a duplicate purchase is made by mistake, or if a technical issue prevents you from accessing your purchased outputs. Since WhatSaid is a digital service with immediate delivery, refunds are generally not available once processing has been completed successfully and outputs are delivered to your account.
refund.s4Body: Refunds are generally not available for successfully processed files where outputs have been delivered, dissatisfaction with AI-generated output quality (as noted in our Terms, AI outputs may contain errors), voluntary account deletion with unused credits, or purchases where the service performed as described.
refund.s5Body: If you are charged twice for the same purchase or experience a payment error, contact us and we will investigate and resolve it promptly. In cases of obvious billing errors, we will issue a correction or refund.
refund.s6Body: Payments are processed securely by Paddle, our merchant of record. Paddle handles billing, tax, receipts, and the refund transaction process on our behalf. Approved refunds are processed through Paddle and returned to the original payment method.
refund.s7Body: Nothing in this policy excludes or limits your statutory consumer rights under applicable law. If you have any questions about this Refund Policy or would like to request a refund, contact us at support@whatsaid.app.

footer.desc: Premium AI transcription with speaker labels, summaries, and custom analysis. Privacy-first — audio deleted after processing.
footer.operator: Operated by Fabio Petito, trading as WhatSaid

pricing.heroTitle: Buy what you use, keep what you get
pricing.heroSubtitle: Each credit unlocks a transcript with speakers, a structured summary and Q&A — all saved in your account, all exportable.
pricing.heroSupport: Create your account in seconds, upload your file, and keep everything accessible whenever you need it.
pricing.valueSubtitle: One credit, three outputs you can read, edit, ask, share and export — no per-minute meter.
pricing.valTranscriptDesc: Speaker-labelled transcript with timestamps, editable any time.
pricing.valSummaryDesc: Key points, decisions and actions — kept in sync with your edits.
pricing.valQuestionsDesc: Ask anything against one recording or across several you own.
pricing.valDownloadDesc: Export your results as text, JSON, DOCX, or PDF — anytime.
pricing.valSavedDesc: Your transcripts, summaries, and downloads stay accessible in your account.
pricing.creditTableTitle: How a credit maps to audio length
pricing.creditTableSub: 1 credit per transcription, up to 120 minutes — longer files cost +1 credit per extra 120-min block.
pricing.creditTableBody: Most recordings cost a single credit. Longer files use one extra credit for each additional 120 minutes.
pricing.creditTableExtra: +1 credit per extra 120 min · max 480 min per file
pricing.cardsTitle: Choose how you want to start
pricing.cardsSubtitle: One-time or credit packs — your results stay saved in your account.
pricing.featureOneCreditPerFile: 1 credit = 1 transcription up to 120 min · longer files use additional credits
pricing.featureSavedAll: Saved history for all processed files
pricing.disclaimer: Displayed in your selected currency. Final amount and any applicable tax are confirmed securely by Paddle at checkout.
pricing.paddleNote: Payments are processed securely by Paddle, our merchant of record.
pricing.consentPrefix: By purchasing, you agree to our
pricing.trustBody: Create your account in seconds so your transcript, summary, downloads, and history stay available whenever you need them.
pricing.trustReason: This is why purchases are account-based — so everything remains accessible and downloadable later.
pricing.howCreditSub: Three steps from upload to results saved in your account.
pricing.howCreditStep1Desc: Drag and drop an .m4a, .mp3, or .wav. We detect the duration and how many credits it needs (1 per 120 min).
pricing.howCreditStep2Desc: We deduct the right number of credits only when processing succeeds.
pricing.howCreditStep3Desc: Transcript, summary and Q&A are saved to your account, ready to read and export.
pricing.faqDesc: The essentials. The Help centre has the full picture, including edge cases.
pricing.faqAccountQ: Do I need an account before buying?
pricing.faqAccountA: Yes. Your account keeps your transcript, summary, downloads, and history saved and accessible whenever you need them.
pricing.faqCreditA: One credit processes one audio file and includes a full transcript, AI summary, and follow-up questions — all downloadable and saved in your account. Longer files use more credits in 120-minute brackets (see the credit table above).
pricing.faqExpireA: Credits remain available in your account based on the product terms.
pricing.faqDownloadQ: Can I download my results later?
pricing.faqDownloadA: Yes. All results stay saved in your account and can be downloaded anytime as TXT, JSON, DOCX, or PDF.
pricing.faqShareQ: Can I share a transcript with someone without an account?
pricing.faqShareA: Yes — you can email a transcript to one recipient. They can download a PDF copy, or claim a copy into their own WhatSaid account if they create one.
pricing.faqSubscriptionA: No. These are one-time purchases. Buy what you need, when you need it.
pricing.faqVariationQ: Why might the checkout amount vary slightly by country?
pricing.faqVariationA: Prices are localised for supported currencies and regions. The final amount, formatting, and any applicable tax are confirmed by Paddle at checkout.
pricing.finalTitle: Choose the option that fits how you work
pricing.finalBody: Start with one file or get credits for repeat use — your results stay saved in your account.
pricing.comingSoon: Payment system is being finalised — check back soon.
pricing.purchaseSuccess: Purchase complete! Your credits have been added.
pricing.moreFaqLink: More questions about credits and billing →
pricing.faqFailedQ: How are credits handled if a job fails?
pricing.faqFailedA: Credits are deducted when processing starts. If a job fails partway through, contact support and we'll refund the credits. Jobs that get stuck for too long are detected automatically and refunded by our system.

transcriptionSettings.title: Transcription settings
transcriptionSettings.strategyBalancedDesc: Standard transcription. Best for clear audio with distinct speakers.
transcriptionSettings.strategyRecoveryDesc: Maximises recovery of faint speech, short interjections, and disfluencies. Best for difficult mono recordings like phone calls.
transcriptionSettings.strategyReviewDesc: Flags unclear audio as [unclear] instead of guessing. Best for QA workflows.
transcriptionSettings.strategyKeytermsDesc: Improve accuracy for specific names, medical terms, or jargon.
transcriptionSettings.keytermsHelp: Enter terms separated by commas. Up to 1,000 words or phrases (max 6 words each).
transcriptionSettings.speakersHelp: Leave on Auto-detect unless you already know how many people are speaking.
transcriptionSettings.enhanceHelp: Reduces volume differences between speakers. Recommended for phone calls recorded on speaker.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.pdfExportStarted: PDF export started — you can navigate freely
notifications.pdfReady: Your PDF is ready to download
notifications.pdfExportFailed: PDF export failed — please try again
notifications.exportAlreadyRunning: This export is already in progress
notifications.pdfReadyCheckNotifications: PDF ready — check notifications to view
notifications.browserPushBody: Your transcript is ready to view.

others.title: Tag review
others.desc: AI-generated tags that may not be in English. Rename, delete, or dismiss.
others.emptyDesc: All recent AI tags look clean.
others.fixConfirmDesc: This will translate {{count}} tag(s) to English and seed the original text as a translation in the detected source language.

help.title: Help & FAQ
help.lead: Quick answers, how WhatSaid works, and what to do when something goes wrong.

mock.demoQ1: How does the credit system work?
mock.demoQ2: What audio formats can I upload?
mock.demoQ3: Is my audio stored after processing?
mock.demoAnswer: 1 credit transcribes up to 120 minutes of audio. Credits never expire and apply to summaries, custom questions, and re-runs at no extra cost.

workflow.lead: From upload to download in a few simple steps.

faq.feedbackNoAria: Mark this answer as not helpful

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.lead: Common issues and what to try first.

privacy.title: Privacy at a glance
privacy.b1: Your audio is deleted right after processing.
privacy.b2: Transcripts and summaries stay only in your account.
privacy.b3: We never sell or share your content.

contact.title: Still need help?
contact.body: Can't find an answer? Reach out — we read every message.

sharedPdf.signInTitle: Sign in to download the PDF
sharedPdf.signInDesc: For security, shared PDFs are only available to logged-in WhatSaid accounts.
sharedPdf.doneDesc: If it did not start automatically, use the button below.
sharedPdf.errorInvalidLink: This PDF link is not valid.

legalBanner.title: Only available in English
legalBanner.body: This document is currently only available in English. The English version is the legally binding one. Reviewed translations are coming soon.

regionBlocked.title: WhatSaid is only available in the United Kingdom
regionBlocked.detailDeclaredNotGb: Please select United Kingdom to continue, or contact us if you believe this is an error.
regionBlocked.detailIpNotGb: Your connection appears to be outside the UK. WhatSaid is currently available to UK residents only.
regionBlocked.detailDefault: Your account is registered to a region we don’t yet support. WhatSaid is currently available to UK residents only.
regionBlocked.vpnAdvice: If you’re in the UK but using a VPN or a network that hides your location, please disable it and try again — or contact support.
